breakthrough: add_gen = 2n discovered via LEdiv+DEML construction: lediv(x, deml(y,1)) = ln(exp(x)/exp(-y)) = x+y for ALL real x,y

construction_detail.combined: LEDIV(x, DEML(y,1)) = ln(exp(x)/exp(-y)) = ln(exp(x)*exp(y)) = ln(exp(x+y)) = x+y

table.domain: x > 0, y != 0 (ln(x) requires x > 0; confirmed X7 audit)
x > 0 (EXL(0,x) = ln(x) undefined for x <= 0; confirmed X7 audit)
table.domain_note: mul=2n holds for x>0. For x<0 no single real-ELC tree exists; branching requires 6n+conditional. abs(x) is outside real ELC (non-differentiable at 0, analytic barrier).

table.notes: NEW v5: all real x,y. Replaces add_pos=3n (positive domain) and add_gen=11n (general). Single unified entry.

improvement: add_gen drops 9n; add_pos drops 1n; positive/general split eliminated — single unified add=2n entry
completeness: All 10 operations <= 3 nodes. Table is COMPLETE.

open_problem_resolved.was: add_gen = 11n (best known); lower bound was open; conjecture was 11n tight (add_gen_11n_conjecture.json)
open_problem_resolved.now: add_gen = 2n PROVED by explicit construction. The 11n construction was not optimal.
open_problem_resolved.implication: Every polynomial-like sum of signed terms drops from 11n to 2n per addition. KL divergence, Nash equilibrium denominator, ELO rating update, Henderson-Hasselbalch, reaction free energy dG all benefit.

linear_cost_law_update.note: The positive/general distinction collapses. All N-term sums now use the unified add=2n cost.
